title: Agent tool-use loop (plan → act → observe → repeat) with guardrails
when: Building an autonomous agent or multi-agent system that calls tools and reacts to results
principle: Loop: the model proposes ONE action (a tool call with args), you execute it, feed the observation back, repeat until a stop condition. Always cap iterations, validate tool args before executing, isolate side effects behind a typed tool interface, and keep a transcript for auditability. For multi-agent, give each agent a single responsibility and a clear input/output contract; converge on an explicit done signal — never loop forever.
exemplar: except Exception as e: obs = f'error: {e}'
pitfalls: Unbounded loops, executing tool args without validation, no transcript, agents with overlapping responsibilities that fight, no explicit termination/convergence.
